data.title: Input Layer and Encoding
data.description: Encode arithmetic expressions and linear equations as directed graphs. No node attributes — identity emerges from structure. Input layer immutable. Seed rules provided as bootstrap scaffold.
data.exit_condition: Expressions reliably converted to base graphs. Sample manually inspected and encoding confirmed sane. ENCODING EXIT CONDITION MET — encoding verified by smoke tests 2026-05-04. Arithmetic rules written as triple graph schema 2026-05-10. Next task: write tests to validate apply() pipeline end-to-end.

encoding.tag_design: Two tag functions: _tag_cycle(node, size) for unfinished state, _tag_cycle_plus(node, size) for finished state. Both include a left anchor node off cycle[0] — provides operand asymmetry and uniform GA grammar. Tail off cycle[-1] in _tag_cycle_plus = finished state marker.
encoding.finished_unfinished: Unfinished = operator can still be reduced (both operands concrete numbers). Arithmetic rules fire on unfinished operators. Finished = terminal, no rules fire. Happens when at least one operand contains a parameter. build_operator(graph, op, finished=False) default.
encoding.numbers: Open-length binary tree. MSB at root, LSB at deepest right leaf. Empty node = 0, self-loop = 1. build_number returns (root, lsb) tuple — lsb known at construction, no traversal needed.
encoding.parameters: Bidirectional structural edge to companion node. build_parameter returns (root, lsb) where both are same node.
encoding.connect_operands: Takes (op_node, left_root, left_lsb, right_root, right_lsb). Position edges attached to LSBs at encode time. Universal — applies to all operators.
encoding.negative_numbers: Represented as 0 - n subtree. No sign node.
encoding.carry_marker: 2-cycle attached to result node via operational edge from result node to one cycle node. Asymmetry from directionality of operational edge — no extra structure needed. On result node, not op node — keeps op node pattern uniform across all bit rules.
encoding.position_pointer: Two operational edges from operator node to current bit position (LSB initially). Advance = position edge moves to parent node. Parent included in pattern — node switching handles reattachment.
encoding.tombstone_marker: Operational self-loop on node. Propagated to structural children by tombstone_gc rule. Simpler than old 3-node chain design.

seed_rules.scope: Arithmetic reduction only. Equation solving rules withheld — parameter isolation is primary discovery target.
taught: Neutral element collapses: x + 0 -> x, x - 0 -> x, x * 1 -> x, x / 1 -> x
Arithmetic reduction rules for + on concrete numbers (written, untested)
withheld: Parameter isolation: moving x to one side of equality
Any rule that operates on equality node to rebalance equation
seed_rules.status: Rewritten as triple graph schema in encoding.py (2026-05-10). Untested.

add_init.description: First bit step. Pattern: op node + left LSB + right LSB + left parent + right parent. No result node in pattern. graph2s: create result node with correct bit tag. graph2o: wire op -> left_parent, op -> right_parent, op -> result. Add carry if needed.

bit_add.description: Mid-reduction bit step. Pattern includes result node. Carry encoded as 2-cycle on result node. graph2s: retag result, remove carry if consumed. graph2o: advance position edges to parents, rewire op -> result, add carry if produced.

drain.description: Fires when one side exhausted (no parent in pattern). 4 rules per side (left/right) × bit × carry = 8 total. Active side advances, exhausted side stays at MSB.

add_finalise.description: Fires when both positions at MSB. Op node recycled as result root — cycle tag nodes removed by step 4b, bare op node inherits parent operational edge. graph2s: connect recycled op to result tree. If carry_out: create extra MSB node (value=1). graph2o: empty.

tombstone_gc.description: Pattern: tombstoned node (operational self-loop) + structural child. graph2s: child gets tombstone, parent removed.

operations_redesign.summary: OperationDefinition holds (pattern, graph2s, graph2o). apply() runs two passes via _apply_pass(). graph2s strips OPERATIONAL outputs STRUCTURAL. graph2o strips STRUCTURAL outputs OPERATIONAL. Step 6 reattaches stripped edges to surviving mapped nodes.
known_limitations: match() is O(n!) via permutations — acceptable for prototype, flagged for P6 profiling
All 9 existing tests broken — need rewriting against new schema
Operand left/right order through match() permutation unverified — must check before subtraction rules

results: Node count for zero: 1, edge count: 0 — correct
3+4 graph: 12 nodes, 15 edges — verified by hand count
x+4=7 graph: 22 nodes, 28 edges — structurally sound

design_decisions_this_session: Operator identity by unique cycle size — no overloading, no tail for identity
Tail = finished state uniformly across all operators and equality
Anchor off cycle[0] on all tags — operand asymmetry + GA grammar consistency
build_number returns (root, lsb) — lsb known at construction
connect_operands attaches to LSBs — universal across all operators
Carry marker on result node not op node — op node pattern stays uniform
Op node recycled as result root in add_finalise — no rewiring problem
Drain rules handle exhaustion by pattern absence of parent — no explicit marker
Wildcard problem deferred to layer transitions — not a P2 concern

next_session.task: Write tests. Validate apply() pipeline end-to-end.
test_priority: add_init_00 — simplest rule, validates full apply() pipeline
bit_add_01_c0 — validates position advance and result tagging
One seed rule — validates survivor node reattachment via step 6
Rewrite all 9 old tests against new schema
open_questions: Verify operand left/right order survives match() permutation — critical before subtraction rules

deferred_decisions: Wildcard matching — deferred to layer transition design
Subtraction, multiplication, division rules — after + validated
